etymology.description: Semantic 文 (wén, "writing") + phonetic 烏 (wū, "crow") -> at, in
etymology.interpretation: The character 於 (yú, "at") is a phono-semantic compound, where 文(wén, "writing") symbolizes the place or point of reference, and 烏 (wū, "crow") provides the phonetic element. This composition emphasizes the concept of location or presence, as designated by its use in contexts indicating position or situation.

examples.chinese: 我们於公园见面(我們於公園見面)。
他於晚上抵达(他於晚上抵達)。
会议将於周二举行(會議將於週二舉行)。
examples.pinyin: Huìyì jiāng yú zhōu èr jǔxíng.
examples.english: The meeting will be held on Tuesday.
